data.task:
Fold the ticket type `spike` into `question` everywhere it is emitted, and extend ADR-0030 to cover it. This is the founder-approved (2026-06-20) execution of Plan §A's second item — retire the spike CONCEPT (the directory genre was already cut in 0e195ef; this removes the residual ticket-type vocabulary). Fold all four live surfaces so no second copy of the type list is left behind:

1. SSOT — packages/daemon/src/routes.ts:
   - line 149: change `type TicketKind = 'bug' | 'task' | 'question' | 'spike'` -> drop `| 'spike'` (=> `'bug' | 'task' | 'question'`).
   - line 172: change `TICKET_TYPES` array `['bug', 'task', 'question', 'spike']` -> `['bug', 'task', 'question']`.
   - line 218: change the error string `'type must be one of bug, task, question, spike'` -> `'type must be one of bug, task, question'`.

2. Base governance — packages/personas/base/plays/create-ticket.md:
   - line 31: enum `must be one of `bug`, `task`, `question`, or `spike`` -> drop `spike` (`bug`, `task`, or `question`).
   - line 24: reword the prose so it no longer lists `spikes` as a ticket genre. It currently reads roughly 'work, faults, questions, or spikes discovered mid-run.' Change to 'work, faults, or questions discovered mid-run.' (a spike-shaped need is now a `question`).

3. Workspace docs — cocoder/tickets/AGENTS.md:
   - line 33: `type: bug | task | question | spike` -> `type: bug | task | question`.
   - line 11: the decision-table row 'A spike or research task to validate an assumption | Yes' — reword so it no longer introduces `spike` as a type; e.g. 'A research question to validate an assumption | Yes' (it maps to `type: question`). Keep the table shape/格式 intact.

4. Workspace docs — cocoder/tickets/INDEX.md:
   - line 9: `Type: `bug | task | question | spike`` -> `Type: `bug | task | question``.

5. EXTEND ADR-0030 (do NOT create ADR-0031). The ADR currently scopes itself to the directory genre only; lines 41-42 read: 'This does **not** change ticket taxonomy. A ticket may still have `type: spike`; that is a ticket kind, ...'. REPLACE that not-changing-taxonomy statement with the fold record: state that this ADR now also retires the `spike` ticket TYPE, folding it into `question` (one concept — directory genre + ticket type — one ADR). Update the ADR title and/or Decision section so the ADR reads as 'retire the spike concept' covering BOTH the directory genre and the ticket type. Add the four folded surfaces (routes.ts SSOT, create-ticket.md, tickets/AGENTS.md, tickets/INDEX.md) to its Consequences/affected-surfaces list. Keep it append-only in spirit — amend, do not rewrite history of the directory-cut portion.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- After the change, grep for the ticket-type token across these four code/governance surfaces returns NO `spike` in any TicketKind union, TICKET_TYPES array, validation error string, create-ticket enum, or tickets docs type line.
- `pnpm -r typecheck` is green (the routes.ts type narrows — confirm no consumer relied on the `spike` member).
- The daemon package tests + any ticket-validation tests stay green: run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` (and `pnpm -r typecheck`). Report exact commands + exit codes.
- `scripts/proof-*.mjs` (run the ones that exist) stay green.
- ADR-0030 now records the ticket-type fold; no ADR-0031 created.

MUST NOT BREAK / OUT OF SCOPE:
- Do NOT touch `packages/adapters/tests/adapters.test.ts` — its `spike` is unrelated (headless-CLI invocation pinning).
- Do NOT alter any of the historical frozen notes under cocoder/zArchive/spikes/.
- Do NOT change other ticket types or unrelated routes logic.
- Do NOT migrate existing ticket files; if any open ticket currently has `type: spike`, report it (do not silently rewrite) — but check `cocoder/tickets/` and flag any.

Report: the full diff, the exact verification commands with exit codes, and any open ticket still carrying `type: spike`.

data.reason: Four mapped surfaces + ADR-0030 folded correctly and typecheck is green, but a fifth live emitter was missed: packages/ui/src/renderer/sections/modals.tsx:133 still offers a 'spike' option (and line 127 prose) that the now-narrowed daemon SSOT would reject — a second copy left behind, violating the atom's acceptance criteria.

data.task:
Complete the `spike` -> `question` ticket-type fold by folding the ONE missed live emitter. Your previous changes from the prior atom are STILL in the working tree (routes.ts, create-ticket.md, tickets/AGENTS.md, tickets/INDEX.md, ADR-0030) and were verified correct — DO NOT revert, undo, or re-touch them. Only add the UI surface below, then re-run the full check so the whole fold lands as one clean commit.

The missed emitter — packages/ui/src/renderer/sections/modals.tsx (the New-ticket modal):

1. line ~133: the Type <select> currently has four <option> children: bug, task, question, spike. REMOVE the `<option value="spike">spike</option>` so only bug/task/question remain — this matches the now-narrowed daemon SSOT (TICKET_TYPES = ['bug','task','question']). The default `type` state value must remain a valid type (it should already default to 'bug' or 'task'; verify it is NOT 'spike').

2. line ~127: the footer help prose reads 'Tickets track bugs, questions, spikes, and follow-up tasks outside the priority queue.' Reword to drop `spikes`: 'Tickets track bugs, questions, and follow-up tasks outside the priority queue.'

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `grep -rn spike packages/ui/src` returns NO ticket-type emitter (no spike <option>, no 'spikes' in the ticket-modal prose).
- The full set of fold surfaces is now clean: grep across packages/daemon/src/routes.ts, packages/personas/base/plays/create-ticket.md, cocoder/tickets/AGENTS.md, cocoder/tickets/INDEX.md, packages/ui/src/renderer/sections/modals.tsx shows NO residual `spike` ticket type.
- `pnpm -r typecheck` green.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` green (run whichever test scripts exist; report exact commands + exit codes).
- Any `scripts/proof-*.mjs` that exist stay green.
- ADR-0030 and the four prior surfaces are UNCHANGED from the prior atom (verify with git diff that you did not re-edit them).

MUST NOT BREAK / OUT OF SCOPE:
- Do NOT revert or re-edit routes.ts, create-ticket.md, tickets/AGENTS.md, tickets/INDEX.md, or ADR-0030 — they are already correct.
- Do NOT touch packages/adapters/tests/adapters.test.ts (unrelated headless-CLI `spike`).
- Do NOT touch cocoder/zArchive/spikes/ frozen notes.
- Do NOT change unrelated modal logic, validation, or other ticket fields.

Report: the modals.tsx diff, the exact verification commands with exit codes, and confirmation (via git diff --stat) that the prior five files were not re-touched.

data.reason: spike fold complete across all five emitters (grep-clean); type default='task'; typecheck exit 0, daemon 236/236, ui 156/156, proof drift-audit+orchestration-enforcer+plays green. The lone red proof (governance-authoring clause E) is a pre-existing stale-title failure asserting retired pre-ADR-0029 guard behavior, provably disjoint from this ticket-type fold.

data.task:
Produce the evidence-backed LOAD-BEARING VERDICT for Plan §B's 'ADR graph' candidate cut. This is Objective verified-when #2 for the founder's stated first-pick candidate. This atom is VERDICT-ONLY analysis written into the priority file — it does NOT execute any collapse, does NOT move/demote/edit/delete any ADR, and does NOT touch runner/daemon/persona code. Executing a collapse is founder-gated (needs a new founder-approved ADR); your job is to give the founder the evidence to decide.

DELIVERABLE: append a new section titled '## §B Verdict — ADR graph' to cocoder/priorities/surface-reduction.md (place it immediately after the existing '### §B Candidate cuts' block, before '## Boundary'). Write it terse, conclusion-first, every claim backed by a cited ADR path/line.

The verdict MUST contain, in order:
1. **Verdict line:** `real` (keep the graph as-is) or `suspect` (collapsible) — one sentence, decisive.
2. **The actual supersession map.** Read every ADR in cocoder/decisions/0001..0030 (note the gaps 0015/0021/0022 — confirm whether those numbers were never issued or were superseded/removed, citing evidence from the README or supersession headers). Build the real chains the Research names: commit-spine (0015→0021→0022→0023→0024→0029) and persona/observation (0013→0016→0017→0026). For EACH ADR classify: `accepted-current` | `superseded` (by which ADR) | `addendum` | `meta` (e.g. 0014 living-adrs). Cite the Status/Supersedes/Superseded-by line of each.
3. **Load-bearing test.** For the candidate proposal — 'a single current-truth surface (ARCHITECTURE.md) that the ADRs feed, so the live graph an agent must read is the accepted set only; superseded ADRs clearly demoted' — determine whether ANYTHING load-bearing currently depends on reading the superseded ADRs directly (search the runner/daemon/persona/Play/proof code and ARCHITECTURE.md for references INTO specific superseded ADR numbers; cite hits). State whether demoting superseded ADRs would weaken any safeguard (verify gate, commit spine, write-scope, founder-vs-agent boundary). Extend the predecessor owner-map method in docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md — reference it; do NOT invent a parallel method.
4. **Concrete sequenced collapse proposal (PROPOSAL ONLY — not executed this atom).** If `suspect`: the exact, reversible, ordered steps to collapse — e.g. which ADRs get a `Superseded` demotion banner, what ARCHITECTURE.md becomes the single current-truth surface for, how an agent navigates 'what is true now' afterward, and what the new founder-approved ADR recording the collapse would assert. Each step names its behavior-pinning check. If `real`: say plainly why the chain is load-bearing and must stay, and stop.
5. **Remaining-suspect follow-ups (Objective #4).** Name the OTHER two §B candidates (persona count 8→fewer; vocabulary/genres beyond spikes) as sequenced, owned follow-ups — one line each on what their verdict atom would examine — so they exit as named work, not unowned intentions. Do NOT verdict them here; just sequence them.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- The new section exists in cocoder/priorities/surface-reduction.md with all five parts, conclusion-first, every ADR-classification claim citing a real file path (and line where applicable).
- ZERO mutation outside cocoder/priorities/surface-reduction.md — confirm via `git status --short` that ONLY that file is modified. No ADR file, no code, no other doc is touched.
- The supersession map covers all 28 numbered ADRs (0001-0030 minus the confirmed gaps) — none silently skipped.
- The load-bearing test cites actual grep evidence (commands + representative hits) for whether code/governance reads superseded ADRs directly.
- No proof/test regression (you changed no code; run `git status --short` to prove it, and note that suites are unaffected).

MUST NOT / OUT OF SCOPE:
- Do NOT move, rename, demote, edit, or delete ANY file in cocoder/decisions/ — this is a verdict, not the collapse. The collapse is a separate founder-approved atom.
- Do NOT edit ARCHITECTURE.md, code, personas, or Plays.
- Do NOT verdict the persona-count or vocabulary candidates (only sequence them as follow-ups).

Report: the appended section's full text, and `git status --short` proving only surface-reduction.md changed.

data.reason: §B ADR-graph verdict appended to surface-reduction.md only (fence clean); all five parts present, conclusion-first; spot-checked citations all accurate (0015/0021/0022→0023 in zArchive+README, 0009→0008, 0011→0013, 0020-addendum→0026, git.ts:10/runner.ts:3 comments real, check-topology.mjs exists); no code touched so suites unaffected.

data.outcome: ✅ COMMITTED on `main` — 3 commit(s) on the active branch (no landing step; work is on the branch by construction). Committed out-of-lane (flagged, NOT held back): cocoder/decisions/0030-retire-spike-genre.md, cocoder/tickets/AGENTS.md, cocoder/tickets/INDEX.md, cocoder/priorities/surface-reduction.md.
